rulings.Case Title: CESAR ASCARRUNZ VS. HOWARD HSU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, January 10, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT HOWARD HSU'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend pursuant to CCP sec. 430.10(e). Per the August 2, 2013 and October 11, 2013 Orders of this Court, the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") does not relate back to the original complaint because additional and different facts were alleged. (This position was asserted by Plaintiff in its Opposition to Motion to Set Aside Default and Quash Service of Summons, filed June 21, 2013, at page 2-3) ("A new 3-year period began to run when the Second Amended Complaint was filed," citing Barrington v. A.H. Robins (1985) 39 Cal. 3d 146).) The Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") relates back to the SAC. Therefore, the date of filing for statute of limitations analysis is the date the SAC was filed, July 28, 2011. The statute of limitations for all causes of action had run by July 28, 2011. (See CCP §§ 337 - 338.) The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend in light of the fact that Plaintiff has already had one opportunity to amend these causes of action and has not demonstrated that these defects can be remedied by amendment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: HENRY WOLF VS. BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton And Moiton For Leave To Augment Expert Disclosures;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 10, 2014, Line 1: PLAINTIFF HENRY WOLF'S Motion For Leave To Augment Expert Disclosures Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. Plaintiff has not shown good cause why the experts already disclosed (including Dr. McAninch's status as a disclosed expert treating physician) are not sufficient for plaintiff's needs at trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EDEN GONZALEA HASS et al VS. RHODYCO PRODUCTIONS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, January 10, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT MEHDI MOOSSAVI MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Grant. No opposition filed. Defendant has met his burden of establishing that Plaintiffs cannot establish their claims for the following reasons: Primary Assumption of the Risk: Cardiovascular stress is an inherent risk of running a half-marathon and Dr. Moossavi did nothing to increase the risk of injury. His declaration establishes that he had no role in planning the event and had no contact with decedent. Waiver: Decedent signed a broad waiver that applied to "any and all claims for damages." The wavier applied to all contractors, employees, representatives, and agents of the event. Dr. Moossavi qualifies as a contractor or agent of the event. He oversaw the Palmer College Chiropractic student volunteers at the event. C. Negligence: Plaintiffs cannot establish that Dr. Moossavi owed a duty to decedent or breached a duty. He did not plan the emergency services and had no contact with decedent. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: BARRY CARTER et al VS. APCO WILLAMETTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Physical And Mental Examination Of Pltf'S Barry Carter
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 10, 2014, Line 2: DEFENDANT M. CHRIS DICKSON REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST, VALVE AND PRIMER CORP. DBA APCO WILLAMETTE'S Motion To Compel Physical And Mental Examination Of Plaintiff Barry Carter Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part, with limitations. Dr. Berg may administer the psychological tests (previously agreed upon by the parties) only, and may not question or interview plaintiff. Dr. Strassberg may conduct a neurologic exam, at which plaintiff's counsel or their representative may be present and record. Dr. Strassberg may also conduct a psychiatric exam, which plaintiff himself may record (no counsel in room). Dr. Strassberg may not repeat questions asked of plaintiff in his deposition, except he may ask about plaintiff's past and present physical/psychological complaints. He may not inquire into sexual history, include sexual abuse by others. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN T. WEST et al VS. LAWRENCE EBRINGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses By Pltfs West To Def Ebringer'S Special Interrogatories Set 3, 7, And 8; Mtn To Compel Further Production Of Documents By Pltf Wests To Def Ebringer'S Document Request Set No. 1
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 10, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANT LAWRENCE EBRINGER'S Motion To Compel Further Responses By Plaintiffs West To Defendant Ebringer's Special Interrogatories Set 3, 7, And 8; Motion To Compel Further Production Of Documents By Plaintiff West To Defendant Ebringer's Document Request Set No. 1 Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part. Plaintiffs are ordered to provide responses to special interrogatories 34-49. The Court finds that the declaration for additional discovery signed by defense counsel substantially complies with CCP sec. 2030.040, and this alleged construction defect case is on its face sufficiently complex to warrant some additional discovery. The remainder of the motion is denied. The Court finds that plaintiffs' responses to interrogatories (except 34-49), combined with their production of thousands of pages of documents, appear adequate to allow the defense to prepare for depositions where it can ask detailed questions about plaintiffs' claims. It is proper to produce documents as they are kept in the ordinary course of business under CCP sec. 2031.280(a). It also appears to the Court that the plaintiffs have made a prima facie showing of work product protection for reports/writings of consultants who have not yet been disclosed as experts. Plaintiffs should not have to produce those documents unless there is a substantial waiver or the consultant is disclosed as an expert under CCP sec. 2034.010 et seq. Sanctions denied (requests by both parties). In addition, the Notice of Motion does not identify party or counsel against whom sanctions are sought. CCP § 2023.040. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CINDY QIWANG XU VS. THE RITZ-CARLTON HOTEL COMPANY, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft The Ritz Carlton Hotel Company, Llc To Provide Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents (Set One) And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 10, 2014, Line 4: PLAINTIFF CINDY XU'S Motion To Compel Defendant The Ritz Carlton Hotel Company, LLC To Provide Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents (Set One) And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part. As to Request for Production (RPD) No. 2, defendant shall produce those portions of Raul Guillermo's personnel file: 1) relating to sexual harassment training or coverage, 2) that mention plaintiff; 3) referring to any claims(s) of sexual harassment against Guillermo including discipline or counseling received. RPD 14, 15, 16: Defendant shall produce the documents demanded, except for privileged documents, for which defendant shall prepare a privilege log stating at a minimum: date, Bates number (if any), preparer, recipient, length, type of document, and grounds for withholding. RPD 17: Defendant shall produce the documents demanded, limited in scope as ordered above for RPD No. 2. RPD 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 33, and 39: Defendant shall produce the documents demanded except for privileged documents, for which defendant shall prepare a privilege log stating at a minimum: date, Bates number (if any), preparer, recipient, length, type of document, and grounds for withholding. Sanctions denied as to both sides. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CINDY QIWANG XU VS. THE RITZ-CARLTON HOTEL COMPANY, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft The Ritz Carlton Hotel Company, Llc To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One) And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 10, 2014, Line 4: PLAINTIFF CINDY XU'S Motion To Compel Defendant The Ritz Carlton Hotel Company, LLC To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One) And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: As to Special Interrogatory No. 1: Defendant shall answer this interrogatory fully. As to former employees (no longer employed by defendant), defendant shall provide last known contact information. As to Special Interrogatory No. 2: Defendant shall answer this interrogatory as to employees working at the hotel from January 1, 2012 onward. As to former employees (no longer employed by defendant), defendant shall provide last known contact information. Sanctions denied as to both sides. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
